search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae dizziness telehealth clinical guideline PDF
site:nice.org.uk NG127 dizziness vertigo recommendations adults
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medicine and treatment content was not mapped.
Rehabilitation recommendations were not treated as generic dizziness documentation evidence.
population_applicability: Partial: the DHA guideline is broad for telehealth dizziness and NICE NG127 is adult-only; the legacy workflow has no age restriction.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for telehealth assessment and adult primary or community referral recognition, not emergency treatment.
UAE_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth assessment, with NICE adult referral guidance requiring UAE clinician review and local pathways.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 dizziness guideline and NICE NG127 adult recommendations, last updated 2023-10-02, were rechecked on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer DHA issue was identified; NG127 remains the current official NICE suspected-neurological-conditions guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped dizziness and vertigo telehealth presentation terms.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth dizziness scope and emergency/controlled-medication exclusions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped neurological, cranial-nerve, nystagmus, gait, Romberg, ear, vital-sign, and assessment fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Emphasises structured history and available examination to distinguish dizziness syndromes.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped focal-neurological, ataxia, syncope, cardiac, occipital-headache, hearing, assessed-negative, and safety fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists cardiac, neurologic, trauma, intoxication, visual, consciousness, and severe-headache concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped symptom type, positional, duration, nausea, vomiting, hearing, headache, trigger, fall, negative, preset, and structured-history fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies symptom description, timing, triggers, concurrent symptoms, age, and pre-existing conditions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped ECG, glucose, postural blood pressure, cardiac monitoring, and hearing-test fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: States that tests depend on history and examination and should be documented only when clinically deemed necessary.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-decided audiology or other referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies emergency and specialist referral features for dizziness.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult Hallpike, HINTS, and brain-imaging referral fields in the specified clinical contexts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports focal neurological and hearing red flags, acute vestibular syndrome, HINTS by trained clinicians, neuroimaging referral, and Hallpike assessment for positional vertigo.

unresolved_source_gaps: Children are not covered by the NICE adult recommendation and emergency treatment remains outside the mapped content.
Vestibular rehabilitation, generic clinician plans, generic follow-up, fixed intervals, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
HINTS is appropriate only for trained clinicians in the specified acute vestibular syndrome context; qualified clinician review remains required.
